content.text: are the two primary text-generation functions in the Vercel AI SDK. Both accept the same options and support tools, conversation history, and system prompts. The difference is purely about
Choosing between them is a UX decision: if the caller needs the full text before doing anything else (classification, data extraction, chain-of-thought that feeds a second call), use
. If the user should see text appear word-by-word in a chat interface, use

attrs.alt: generateText vs streamText data flow: generateText buffers the full response; streamText yields tokens progressively via AsyncIterable

content.text: sends the request and awaits the LLM's complete output before returning. The result object is synchronously readable — no async iteration needed:
Prompt, completion, and total token counts
Any tool calls the model made (if tools were provided)
Results of executed tool calls (if execute was provided)
